title: User cannot access a shared folder

ticket.description: The user receives Access Denied when opening the Projects shared folder they should be able to use.

actions.label: Ask whether the user can access other network resources
actions.result: The user can reach the intranet and other shares, but only the Projects share returns Access Denied.

evidence.title: Scope
evidence.body: Network connectivity works; the failure is isolated to one shared folder.

actions.label: Verify the user's identity before changing access
actions.result: The user's identity is verified through the approved help desk process.

actions.label: Confirm the correct UNC path and department need
actions.result: The correct path is \FS01\Projects and the user is assigned to the Projects department.

evidence.title: Share Path
evidence.body: The user is trying to access the correct approved share path.

actions.label: Check AD group membership for the Projects share
actions.result: The user is missing the Projects-Share-RW group required for access.

evidence.title: Permission Root Cause
evidence.body: The account is missing the security group used to grant share access.

actions.label: Add the user to the approved Projects share group
actions.result: The user is added to the approved group after confirming business need.

actions.label: Have the user sign out/in and verify shared folder access
actions.result: After refreshing the logon token, the user can open the Projects share and create a test file.

evidence.title: Verification
evidence.body: The user successfully accessed the share after approved group membership was corrected.

actions.label: Document identity verification, missing group, fix, and verification
actions.result: Ticket notes include the access request validation, group correction, and user verification.

actions.label: Grant Everyone Full Control on the share
actions.result: This would expose the share to unauthorized users.

penalty.reason: Broad permissions violate least privilege and are not an appropriate fix.

actions.label: Map a different drive letter without checking permissions
actions.result: Mapping a drive letter does not resolve missing share permissions.

penalty.reason: The issue is authorization, not the local drive letter.

grading.summary: A strong permissions workflow scopes the issue, verifies identity and business need, checks the correct share/group, applies least-privilege access, verifies, and documents the change.

ticket.description: The user accidentally deleted a document from a shared folder and needs the latest recoverable copy restored.

actions.label: Ask for the file name, location, and last known good time
actions.result: The file was in \FS01\Legal\Contracts and was last known good yesterday afternoon.

evidence.title: Restore Scope
evidence.body: The user identified the file path and approximate restore point needed.

actions.label: Check the approved backup or previous-version source
actions.result: A successful backup exists from last night for the shared folder.

evidence.title: Backup Found
evidence.body: A recent successful backup is available for the affected shared folder.

actions.label: Select the latest known-good copy without overwriting newer unrelated files
actions.result: The latest known-good file version is selected for item-level restore.

actions.label: Restore the file and preserve permissions
actions.result: The file is restored with the expected folder permissions intact.

actions.label: Have the user open the restored file and verify contents
actions.result: The user confirms the restored file opens and contains the expected content.

evidence.title: Verification
evidence.body: The restored document was opened and verified by the user.

actions.label: Document restore source, time, result, and verification
actions.result: Ticket notes record the backup source, restore time, file restored, and user verification.

actions.label: Restore the entire Legal share over the current folder
actions.result: This could overwrite newer files that were not affected.

penalty.reason: An entire-share restore is too broad for a single deleted file.

actions.label: Close the ticket after starting the restore without user verification
actions.result: The ticket is closed before proving the correct file was restored.

penalty.reason: Restore jobs must be verified before closing the ticket.

grading.summary: A good restore workflow scopes the deleted item, finds a valid backup, restores only what is needed, verifies with the user, and documents the result.

title: Remote user cannot connect to VPN

ticket.description: A remote user cannot connect to the corporate VPN before a customer meeting.

actions.label: Ask for the VPN error and whether internet access works
actions.result: Websites load normally. VPN reports that the MFA push was denied or expired.

evidence.title: VPN Error
evidence.body: The VPN failure occurs during MFA, not during basic internet connectivity.

actions.label: Verify internet access outside the VPN
actions.result: The laptop can browse external websites and resolve DNS normally.

actions.label: Verify the user account is active and not locked
actions.result: The account is active, password is valid, and there are no lockouts.

actions.label: Check MFA registration and recent authentication attempts
actions.result: The user's old phone is still registered for MFA. Push attempts are going to the wrong device.

evidence.title: MFA Root Cause
evidence.body: MFA prompts are being sent to the user's old registered device.

actions.label: Reset MFA registration using the approved identity process
actions.result: After verifying identity, MFA is re-registered on the user's current phone.

actions.label: Verify the user completes MFA and connects to VPN
actions.result: The user approves MFA on the current phone and connects to the VPN successfully.

evidence.title: Verification
evidence.body: VPN connection succeeded after approved MFA re-registration.

actions.label: Document MFA root cause, reset process, and VPN verification
actions.result: Ticket notes include the VPN error, MFA registration correction, and successful connection test.

actions.label: Disable MFA so the user can connect immediately
actions.result: Disabling MFA weakens remote-access security.

penalty.reason: MFA should not be bypassed for convenience; fix the enrollment issue securely.

actions.label: Reinstall the VPN client before checking the error
actions.result: The VPN client was not the root cause because authentication was reaching MFA.

penalty.reason: The evidence points to MFA registration, not a broken VPN client.

grading.summary: A strong VPN workflow separates internet connectivity from VPN authentication, checks account and MFA state, fixes MFA securely, verifies connection, and documents the ticket.

ticket.description: The user received a phone call from someone claiming to be IT and asking for their password and MFA code.

actions.label: Tell the user not to share credentials or MFA codes
actions.result: The user ends the call and confirms they will not share credentials or MFA codes.

actions.label: Collect caller number, time, request, and any links or callback numbers
actions.result: The caller spoofed a help desk number and requested the user's password and MFA approval.

evidence.title: Incident Details
evidence.body: The call used IT impersonation and credential/MFA pressure, consistent with vishing.

actions.label: Confirm whether the user disclosed password or approved MFA
actions.result: The user did not disclose the password and did not approve an MFA prompt.

actions.label: Report the incident to the security team according to procedure
actions.result: The security team receives the incident details for investigation and awareness.

actions.label: Check for suspicious sign-ins and reset credentials only if policy requires
actions.result: No suspicious sign-ins are found. The account is monitored and the user is advised to report any MFA prompts.

evidence.title: Account Check
evidence.body: No evidence of account compromise was found after the reported vishing call.

actions.label: Educate the user on vishing, MFA fatigue, and reporting procedures
actions.result: The user understands that IT will not ask for passwords or MFA codes.

actions.label: Document reported vishing attempt, escalation, account check, and education
actions.result: Ticket documents the incident details, security escalation, account check, and user education.

actions.label: Ask the user for their password to test the account
actions.result: This repeats the attacker's unsafe request.

penalty.reason: Technicians should never ask users to disclose passwords.

actions.label: Close the ticket because the user did not share the password
actions.result: Potential social engineering attempts still need reporting and documentation.

penalty.reason: Security incidents should be reported even if compromise was avoided.

grading.summary: A strong social-engineering response stops credential sharing, collects details, checks for compromise, escalates, educates the user, and documents the incident.

ticket.description: Monthly Windows updates fail repeatedly with a storage-related error.

actions.label: Review Windows Update history and error details
actions.result: Update history shows repeated failures with an error indicating insufficient free space.

evidence.title: Update Error
evidence.body: The failure code and update history point to insufficient disk space.

actions.label: Check free disk space on the system drive
actions.result: The C: drive has less than 2 GB free.

actions.label: Free space using Disk Cleanup or Storage Sense
actions.result: Temporary files and old update cleanup free more than 12 GB of disk space.

evidence.title: Disk Space
evidence.body: Enough free space is available for the update after cleanup.

actions.label: Retry Windows Update after freeing space
actions.result: The update downloads and installs successfully.

actions.label: Restart and verify Windows Update reports current status
actions.result: After restart, Windows Update shows the device is up to date.

evidence.title: Verification
evidence.body: Windows Update completed successfully and reports current status.

actions.label: Document update error, cleanup, successful install, and verification
actions.result: Ticket documents the error, disk cleanup, successful update, and final verification.

actions.label: Disable Windows Update to stop the errors
actions.result: This leaves the workstation without security updates.

penalty.reason: Disabling updates is unsafe and does not fix the underlying storage issue.

actions.label: Reimage the workstation before checking the error
actions.result: A reimage is unnecessary before reviewing the update error and disk space.

penalty.reason: Reimaging is too invasive for an update failure caused by low disk space.

grading.summary: A good Windows Update workflow reviews the error, checks disk space, frees space safely, retries the update, verifies after reboot, and documents the result.
